title: The Endowment Effect: You're Holding a $9,200 Mistake
hook: Your brain triples the value of anything you own — and that's keeping you in losing stocks, overpriced homes, and underpaid jobs.
angle: The ownership illusion is a well-documented bias (Kahneman, Ariely) but almost nobody frames it as a dollar-amount financial trap across investing, real estate, and careers simultaneously.
audience: Anyone who has ever held a losing investment too long, overpriced something they sold, or stayed at a job past its prime
key_numbers: 3× — brain's average markup on things you own
$9,200 — average retail investor's trapped losing position
71% — traders who hold losses 2× longer than winners
22% — how much home sellers overprice on average
50% — lifetime earnings gap between loyal employees and job switchers
